donutSubtitle: How well your brand is aligned in AI responses.
donutEmpty: No perception signal available for the selected filters.
heatmapDescription: Compare which models understand each perception axis best.
heatmapEmpty: No model comparison available for the selected filters.
trendDescription: Track positioning clarity, factual reliability, and response tone over {{periodLabel}} to measure optimization impact.
trendEmpty: No trend data available for the selected period.
trendDefinitionPositioning: How well AI can link the brand to its category and core promise.
trendDefinitionFactual: How reliable and verifiable the information used by AI remains.
trendDefinitionSentiment: The overall tone AI uses when talking about your brand, from negative to positive.
scoreCardPositioningHint: Current average score out of 100 for how clearly AI associates the brand with its positioning
scoreCardFactualHint: Average score out of 100 for how reliable and verifiable the information remains
scoreCardSentimentHint: Average score out of 100 for the tone AI uses when talking about your brand
sourceSummaryModePerception: Primary KPI cards on this page are powered by dedicated perception analysis responses.
sourceSummaryModeFallback: No recent dedicated perception response is available yet, so the page is temporarily using monitoring responses as context.
optimizeActionsEmpty: No generated action yet. Click `Create action` in the right column.
optimizeActionsCreateError: Unable to create the optimization action
topErrorsSheetDescription: Details about the detected error and the recommended optimization action.
topErrorsFallbackIssue: The backend reported a perception gap on this axis.
topErrorsFallbackImpact: This gap reduces how well AI understands the brand.
topErrorsFallbackGeneratedContent: Clarify {{brand}} on this axis across the website and source content.
topErrorsGeneratedPositioningTitle: Positioning is still not cited clearly enough
topErrorsGeneratedPositioningIssue: {{score}}% of responses correctly cite {{brand}}. Some models still fail to connect the brand to the right context.
topErrorsGeneratedPositioningImpact: The brand may be under-selected or show up off-topic in AI recommendations.
topErrorsGeneratedPositioningContent: Strengthen the value proposition and use cases directly in landing pages, headings, and FAQs.
topErrorsGeneratedCitationIssue: {{score}}% of responses contain a usable citation. AI often responds without explicit support from your pages.
topErrorsGeneratedCitationImpact: The narrative is less verifiable and the brand feels less factual in results.
topErrorsGeneratedCitationContent: Add FAQ blocks, proof points, statistics, and reference pages that are easy to cite on key product topics.
topErrorsGeneratedUseCaseIssue: {{score}}% of covered prompts lead to a clear mention of {{brand}}. AI still does not connect the brand strongly enough to target scenarios.
topErrorsGeneratedUseCaseImpact: Visibility opportunities on intent-driven queries remain partial.
topErrorsGeneratedUseCaseContent: Make priority use cases more visible in navigation, hero sections, and comparison pages.
topErrorsGeneratedSentimentTitle: Sentiment is still too neutral or negative
topErrorsGeneratedSentimentIssue: Sentiment score is {{score}}/100. Responses still lack differentiating signals or lean too much toward alternatives.
topErrorsGeneratedSentimentImpact: The brand is less convincing in AI summaries and loses desirability.
topErrorsGeneratedSentimentContent: Document value proof, customer outcomes, and differentiators more clearly to steer responses toward stronger positive signals.
topErrorsGeneratedCompetitiveIssue: Competitive score is {{score}}/100. AI still places the brand behind competitors too often in comparisons.
topErrorsGeneratedCompetitiveImpact: Consideration traffic can be captured by other players on comparison queries.
topErrorsGeneratedCompetitiveContent: Add comparison pages, differentiation tables, and proof content about the project's real competitors.
confirmAnalysisDescriptionWithQuota: This analysis will use about {{credits}} credits depending on the active models. Current balance: {{remaining}}/{{total}} credits remaining.
confirmAnalysisDescriptionWithoutQuota: This analysis will use about {{credits}} credits depending on the active models.
analysisInsufficientCreditsDescription: This analysis costs {{credits}} credits. {{remaining}}/{{total}} credits remain in your monthly quota.
errorTypeMentionDrop: The brand is mentioned less often
errorTypeMonitoringVisibilityGap: The brand appears too rarely in tracked prompts
errorTypeMonitoringCitationGap: The brand is mentioned without enough proof
errorTypeMonitoringRankingGap: The brand ranks too low in comparisons
errorTypeMonitoringModelVolatility: Models tell inconsistent stories about the brand
generatedContentPerceptionPositioningGap: Clarify the value proposition, category, and target scenarios across entry pages and FAQs.
generatedContentPerceptionCitationGap: Add proof points, figures, FAQs, and reference content that AI can quote directly on key topics.
generatedContentPerceptionUseCaseGap: Make priority use cases more visible in navigation, hero sections, and comparison pages.
generatedContentPerceptionSentimentGap: Strengthen value proof, customer outcomes, and differentiators across source content.
generatedContentPerceptionCompetitiveGap: Add comparison pages, differentiation tables, and proof content against major alternatives.
generatedContentMonitoringAlert: Review the prompts, recent responses, and sources that are triggering this alert.
generatedContentMonitoringVisibilityGap: Rework core market prompts and strengthen positioning pages and AI-cited proof.
generatedContentMonitoringCitationGap: Add more quotable content: FAQs, comparisons, figures, product proof, and reference pages.
generatedContentMonitoringRankingGap: Clarify the value proposition, differentiators, and competitive comparisons on key pages.
generatedContentMonitoringNegativeShift: Strengthen reassurance content, customer stories, proof of results, and handled objections.
generatedContentMonitoringModelVolatilit: Align positioning content, use cases, and comparisons to reduce model-to-model gaps.
topErrorsMonitoringVisibilityIssue: Mention rate remains too low across priority monitoring prompts.
topErrorsMonitoringVisibilityImpact: The brand may be missing from AI recommendations at key intent moments.
topErrorsMonitoringCitationIssue: AI still cites too few proofs or trustworthy URLs when talking about the brand.
topErrorsMonitoringCitationImpact: The brand's credibility remains fragile in AI answers and comparisons.
topErrorsMonitoringRankingIssue: Responses mention the brand but place it too rarely at the top and too often near the bottom.
topErrorsMonitoringRankingImpact: AI visibility becomes less competitive on high-intent prompts.
topErrorsMonitoringNegativeShiftIssue: Too large a share of monitoring responses talks about the brand with negative tone.
topErrorsMonitoringNegativeShiftImpact: Desirability and trust decline in AI recommendations and comparisons.
topErrorsMonitoringModelVolatilityIssue: Monitoring performance varies too much from one model to another, signaling unstable positioning.
topErrorsMonitoringModelVolatilityImpact: The brand can look strong on some assistants and weak on others, reducing overall consistency.
crawlerIssueFixHttpStatusIssue: The page cannot be analyzed correctly while it returns an error or incomplete status.
crawlerIssueFixHttpStatusImpact: Until {{resource}} returns a stable 200 page, search engines and AI assistants cannot reliably read, rank, or cite its content.
crawlerIssueFixHttpStatusFix: Check the page, restore a 200 status, and remove unnecessary redirects before rerunning analysis.
crawlerIssueAddTitleIssue: The crawl did not find a usable title for this page.
crawlerIssueAddTitleImpact: Without a usable title, {{resource}} sends a weak signal about its main topic and may be misranked or poorly summarized by AI.
crawlerIssueAddTitleFix: Add a unique SEO title that names the offer or topic, target audience, and primary benefit in 45 to 60 characters.
crawlerIssueImproveTitleIssue: The title exists, but its length or framing limits performance in search results.
crawlerIssueImproveTitleImpact: A vague or overlong title weakens {{resource}}'s intent signal and can make the page appear for the wrong prompts or queries.
crawlerIssueImproveTitleFix: Rewrite the title to around 45 to 60 characters with the category, priority use case, and a verifiable promise.
crawlerIssueAddMetaDescriptionIssue: No usable meta description was detected in the crawled HTML.
crawlerIssueAddMetaDescriptionImpact: Without a description, {{resource}} leaves engines to generate an approximate summary, which can weaken click-through and offer comprehension.
crawlerIssueAddMetaDescriptionFix: Add a 140 to 160 character meta description covering the problem addressed, primary proof, and next step.
crawlerIssueExpandContentIssue: The page gives too little context to search engines and AI answers.
crawlerIssueExpandContentImpact: {{resource}} may look insufficient for a complete intent, making it less citeable in AI answers and less competitive in search.
crawlerIssueExpandContentFix: Expand the page with problem framing, benefits, use cases, quantified proof, common objections, and internal links to supporting pages.
crawlerIssueAddTopicDepthIssue: The page covers the topic but remains too shallow for reliable, citeable AI answers.
crawlerIssueAddTopicDepthImpact: AI assistants may prefer competitors that explain the context, limits, and decision criteria around {{resource}}'s topic more clearly.
crawlerIssueAddTopicDepthFix: Add deeper sections: detailed use cases, selection criteria, limitations, alternatives, and sourced proof.
crawlerIssueImproveH1Issue: The page should expose one clear H1 that confirms the main topic.
crawlerIssueImproveH1Impact: A missing or ambiguous H1 blurs {{resource}}'s main topic and makes it harder to match the page to the right search intent.
crawlerIssueImproveH1Fix: Add one descriptive H1 focused on the main topic, audience, or use case without mechanically duplicating the title.
crawlerIssueImproveHeadingsIssue: The page lacks readable subsections for crawling and AI summarization.
crawlerIssueImproveHeadingsImpact: Without clear hierarchy, {{resource}} is harder for generative engines to segment, cite, and turn into a structured answer.
crawlerIssueImproveHeadingsFix: Structure the page with explicit H2 sections for audience, problem, benefits, proof, comparison, objections, and FAQ.
crawlerIssueAddInternalLinksIssue: No useful internal links were detected in the extracted content.
crawlerIssueAddInternalLinksImpact: {{resource}} stays isolated: crawlers and AI assistants have fewer paths to connect the offer to proof, comparisons, and conversion pages.
crawlerIssueAddInternalLinksFix: Add 3 to 5 contextual internal links to an FAQ, comparison page, customer proof, related guide, and primary conversion page.
crawlerIssueAddFaqIssue: Generative engines reuse pages better when they answer user questions directly.
crawlerIssueAddFaqImpact: Without an FAQ, {{resource}} lacks short reusable answers for AI assistants on selection, usage, and comparison questions.
crawlerIssueAddFaqFix: Add 5 to 8 targeted questions and answers covering selection criteria, integration, pricing or effort, limitations, alternatives, and next steps.
crawlerIssueAddDirectAnswerIssue: Generative engines prefer pages that give a concise answer before the details.
crawlerIssueAddDirectAnswerImpact: {{resource}} forces AI systems to infer the main answer, increasing the risk of imprecise summaries or citations from another source.
crawlerIssueAddDirectAnswerFix: Add a 40 to 60 word answer block near the top that directly answers the primary intent before the details.
crawlerIssueAddEvidenceIssue: The content lacks numbers, sources, testimonials, or proof that make AI answers verifiable.
crawlerIssueAddEvidenceImpact: Without verifiable proof, {{resource}} is less likely to be cited as a trustworthy source in AI answers and comparisons.
crawlerIssueAddEvidenceFix: Add concrete proof: dated metrics, customer cases, screenshots or cited sources, methodology, and measurable outcomes.
crawlerIssueAddEntityContextIssue: The page does not provide enough context about the entity, offer, sector, or target audience.
crawlerIssueAddEntityContextImpact: AI systems may connect {{resource}} to the wrong brand, category, audience, or value proposition.
crawlerIssueAddEntityContextFix: Add an entity block naming the brand, category, target audience, sector, use cases, and differentiators.
crawlerIssueClarifyOfferIssue: The page does not clearly describe what is offered, with which features, and which benefits.
crawlerIssueClarifyOfferImpact: If the offer remains unclear, AI assistants may recommend {{resource}} for the wrong needs or prefer clearer competitors.
crawlerIssueClarifyOfferFix: Add an offer block with the promise, key features, benefits by audience, prerequisites, and the situations where the offer fits best.
crawlerIssueAddAudienceUseCasesTitle: Audience and use cases are incomplete
crawlerIssueAddAudienceUseCasesIssue: The page does not give enough context on target users and the situations where the offer should be recommended.
crawlerIssueAddAudienceUseCasesImpact: {{resource}} lacks signals that map it to the right buyer profiles, jobs, or usage situations in AI answers.
crawlerIssueAddAudienceUseCasesFix: Add a “Who it’s for” section with 3 to 5 target audiences, their pains, concrete use cases, and success criteria.
crawlerIssueAddComparisonContextIssue: Generative engines recommend an offer better when choice criteria and alternatives are explicit.
crawlerIssueAddComparisonContextImpact: Without comparison criteria, {{resource}} may be excluded from recommendations where the user asks for alternatives or solution tradeoffs.
crawlerIssueAddComparisonContextFix: Add a comparison block explaining when to choose this offer, when to choose an alternative, and which criteria to evaluate.
crawlerIssueAddFreshnessSignalIssue: The page does not provide a clear signal about update date or recency of information.
crawlerIssueAddFreshnessSignalImpact: AI systems may hesitate to cite {{resource}} for evolving topics if freshness, version, or information date is not visible.
crawlerIssueAddFreshnessSignalFix: Add a visible update date, version, or freshness note, then verify that figures and examples are current.
crawlerIssueAddSchemaMarkupIssue: The HTML does not show usable structured markup for machine understanding.
crawlerIssueAddSchemaMarkupImpact: Without structured data, {{resource}} sends fewer machine-readable signals about the entity, offer, questions, article, or proof available.
crawlerIssueAddSchemaMarkupFix: Add relevant schema.org markup: Organization, Product or Service, FAQPage, Article, or Breadcrumb depending on the real page content.
crawlerIssueCreateBlogIssue: The page lacks supporting content that reinforces authority, comparison, and citeability signals.
crawlerIssueCreateBlogImpact: {{resource}} lacks editorial support to prove expertise, cover long-tail questions, and capture consideration-stage queries.
crawlerIssueCreateBlogFix: Create a linked editorial cluster: buying guide, comparison, use case, in-depth FAQ, and proof article, each linking back to the main page.
brandCanonUnavailableDescription: Edit the brand profile: category, use cases, features, and competitors.
